description: Rigorous scoring rubric for lever coverage completeness and benchmark authenticity. Each dimension is independently computable from platform reference files.

domains.name: lever_coverage
domains.description: How exhaustive, structurally correct, and internally consistent the cost-reduction lever taxonomy is.

dimensions.name: Universal Lever Count & Family Completeness
dimensions.description: Library should have ≥40 levers covering all 6 families (supply, demand, technology, finance, process, structure). Orphaned lever IDs in savings_type_by_lever without a levers{} definition block deduct 0.5 each.

dimensions.name: Lever Structural Integrity
dimensions.description: Every lever must have all 12 required fields. Phasing curves must sum to 1.0 ± 0.001. Float fields must be in [0,1]. implementation_weeks must satisfy p10 ≤ p50 ≤ p90.

dimensions.name: Sector Pack Count & Architecture Parity
dimensions.description: 14 packs have sector_levers.json in skills/sector-packs/. Only packs that ALSO have a pack_manifest.yaml in sector_packs/ are architecturally complete. Score = intersection / skills_packs * 10.

dimensions.name: Full-Pack Artifact Completeness
dimensions.description: Packs with status='full' in pack_manifest.yaml must have all 6 artifacts: sector_levers.json, benchmark_sources.yaml, kpi_pack.json, regulatory_layer.md, peer_set.json, taxonomy_extension.json. Score = mean artifact fraction across full packs.

dimensions.name: Sector Lever Structural Integrity
dimensions.description: Each sector-specific lever must have all 13 required fields. savings_range_pct must have p10/p50/p90 with p10≤p50≤p90. implementation_weeks must have p10≤p50≤p90. phasing_curve must sum to 1.0.

dimensions.name: Sector Lever ID Global Uniqueness
dimensions.description: Lever IDs appearing in multiple sector packs without explicit cross-sector metadata create dispatch ambiguity. Deduct 0.5 per duplicate pair (capped at 5 pairs = max deduction 2.5).

dimensions.name: Universal Lever Cross-Reference Validity
dimensions.description: Each sector pack's universal_levers list references IDs that must resolve to entries in model_parameters.json levers{}. Dangling references indicate maintenance drift.

dimensions.name: Mutual Exclusion & Dependency DAG
dimensions.description: Two structural gaps: (1) No mutually_exclusive_with field prevents insourcing+outsourcing from being simultaneously recommended for the same category. (2) No depends_on field means automation can be recommended without ensuring process_standardization precedes it. Each field contributes 5 points.

domains.name: benchmark_authenticity
domains.description: How credibly sourced, attributed, and taxonomically complete the benchmark data is. Source attribution is weighted highest — unattributed benchmarks cannot be defended in a client engagement.

dimensions.name: Industry Benchmark Source Attribution Quality
dimensions.description: 4-level rubric applied to industry_benchmarks.json: 0=no source named; 3=source named; 6=source+publication type; 9=source+title+year; 10=all+per-category attribution. Current state: Gartner/Hackett named in description but no titles, years, or per-category fields.
dimensions.scoring_method: score_map[rubric_level] where rubric_level = sum(named_source, pub_type_keyword, year_regex_match, per_category_source_field)

dimensions.name: Heuristic Targets Source Attribution
dimensions.description: heuristic_targets.json has no source field and no currency denomination. Per-employee targets (HR:2500, IT:5000) are numerically ambiguous — in USD they overstate cost ~83x vs INR. Both failures are blocking for global deployment.

dimensions.name: DPO Benchmark Source Attribution
dimensions.description: dpo_benchmarks.json lacks a named source. Per-category notes exist and provide analytical context (+2 bonus). DPO figures are sensitive commercial data typically sourced from Hackett AP Benchmarking or Aberdeen.

dimensions.name: Diagnostic Threshold Source Attribution
dimensions.description: diagnostic_thresholds.json contains 5 threshold values and 4 addressable rates — 9 total numeric claims. None have a companion rationale, source, or reference sub-field. The HHI threshold of 0.15 is defensible (DOJ/FTC guidelines use 0.25); maverick ratio of 20% and cost-per-transaction of ₹1000 are unattributed.

dimensions.name: Industry Benchmark Taxonomy Coverage
dimensions.description: Measures what fraction of addressable taxonomy categories have peer benchmark data. Addressable = all 24 categories minus statutory non-addressable (GST_TAX, CSR) minus catch-all (OTHER). Currently 10 of 21 addressable categories have benchmarks — 14 are missing including commercially significant India categories (POWER_ENERGY, PLANT_MAINTENANCE, LOGISTICS_INDIA).

dimensions.name: Heuristic Target Taxonomy Coverage
dimensions.description: Measures what fraction of addressable taxonomy categories have a heuristic % of revenue target. Uses same addressable set as BA_05. Currently 13 of 21 addressable categories covered.

dimensions.name: Licensed Benchmark Stub Transparency
dimensions.description: 2 full packs cite CMIE Prowess IQ / Capitaline Plus as licensed stubs. Score measures: transparency (stubs clearly labelled, not falsely claimed as populated), adapter infrastructure (CmieAdapter class exists), and breadth (what % of packs have any benchmark_sources.yaml).
